content: Working memory capacity limits that constrain agent reasoning do not represent a hard architectural constraint to overcome, but instead act as a built-in implicit curiosity signal that can be leveraged to improve active learning sample selection. Agents that hit working memory bottlenecks when processing partial environmental information will naturally prioritize collecting new observations that resolve the unencoded uncertainty stuck at their capacity limit, acting as a low-overhead intrinsic reward for active learning.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
